title: 10 best herbal creams for acne

remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil's Antimicrobial Properties: Tea tree oil has potent antimicrobial properties that help to reduce the growth of bacteria that can cause acne, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes).
Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Many herbal teas, such as chamomile and calendula, contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Antioxidant Properties: Antioxidants in herbal teas like green tea and peppermint can help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to acne development.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with acne because it contains antimicrobial properties that effectively target and eliminate the bacteria that cause acne. The oil extracted from the leaves of the Melaleuca plant has been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent the spread of acne-causing bacteria, such as Propionibacterium acnes. This natural remedy promotes a healthy balance of skin flora, reducing acne severity and promoting clear, smooth skin. Regular use of tea tree oil creams can lead to long-term acne management and prevention.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene has antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria that cause acne, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes).
Catechins: These phenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent oxidative stress in acne-prone skin, leading to reduced acne severity.
Linalool: This terpene has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria and inflammation in acne-prone skin, thereby preventing acne breakouts.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high content of tea tree oil, which has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat acne.
Flowers: The flowers are also used for their tea tree oil, which has similar properties to the leaves and helps to reduce acne and inflammation.
Buds: The buds contain a higher concentration of tea tree oil than the leaves, making them a popular choice for acne creams due to their potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of melaleuca alternifolia leaves at dawn when the dew is still present on the leaves.
Steam distill the harvested leaves for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil from the plant material.
Mix 10 grams of the distilled essential oil with 100 grams of a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Add 2 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of shea butter to the mixture and heat it gently for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Pot marigold contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation associated with acne, promoting a clearer complexion.

Wound-Healing and Antimicrobial Activity: The cream's antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to acne, while its wound-healing properties help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing the risk of scarring.

Antioxidant and Immune-Strengthening Properties: Pot marigold's antioxidant compounds also help boost the immune system, which can aid in fighting off underlying infections and promoting overall skin health, reducing the likelihood of acne breakouts.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, work to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. Calendula cream's soothing and moisturizing effects also help to calm irritated skin, promoting a healthy balance of the skin's natural flora. By reducing inflammation and bacterial growth, Calendula creams create an ideal environment for acne-prone skin to heal and recover.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce acne severity by controlling bacterial growth and inflammation.
Flavonoids (specifically quercetin): As powerful antioxidants, flavonoids can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with acne, promoting a healthier skin environment.

N-alkanoyl-4-hydroxyphenylalanine amide (also known as CAPE): This compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce redness and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce acne and soothe the skin.
Leaves: They possess antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which help to combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation.
Stems: They contain flavonoids and triterpenoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce acne and promote skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried calendula officinalis flowers and sift them to remove any debris.
Infuse 20g of the dried flowers in 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Mix the infused oil with 10g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture until it reaches a temperature of 180f and then pour it into a jar.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and alocemide, help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, promoting faster healing and reducing scarring.

Antibacterial properties: Aloe vera contains various antibiotics, including lupeol and salicylic acid, which help combat bacteria like Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) that can contribute to acne development, reducing the severity of breakouts.

Hydrating and soothing properties: Aloe vera's moisturizing compounds, such as glycoproteins and mucilages, hydrate and calm the skin, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by acne, and helping to unclog pores.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin that have been shown to reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne. It also has soothing and moisturizing effects, which can help to calm and hydrate the skin, reducing the appearance of acne and promoting a healthy skin balance. Additionally, aloe vera's natural antiseptic properties can help to combat bacterial overgrowth, further reducing acne symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Bradykininase: It helps with acne by inhibiting the production of bradykinin, a peptide that causes inflammation and increases skin sensitivity.
Vitamins A and E (Carotenoids): They help with acne by reducing inflammation, oxidative stress, and skin cell damage, while promoting skin cell regeneration and collagen production.

Polysaccharides (Glucomannans): They help with acne by reducing inflammation, promoting skin cell regeneration, and soothing irritated skin, while also exhibiting antimicrobial properties to prevent bacterial overgrowth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel extracted from the leaves is rich in anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help soothe and heal acne-prone skin.
Leaves: The juice from the leaves contains vitamins and minerals like vitamin E and zinc, which help to reduce inflammation and promote skin healing.
Leaves: The gel from the leaves has anti-aging properties, which can help to reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles associated with acne scars.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of pure aloe vera gel in a stainless steel bowl and set it aside for 10 minutes.
Combine 50 grams of coconut oil and 30 grams of beeswax in a double boiler and melt them slowly over low heat for 15 minutes.
Gradually add 20 grams of shea butter to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture and stir well.
Combine the aloe vera gel with 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of jojoba oil in a separate bowl.
Slowly pour the aloe vera mixture into the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture and whip until smooth and creamy.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Neem's antibacterial property helps to kill P. acnes bacteria, which is a common cause of acne, reducing inflammation and preventing further breakouts.
Anti-inflammatory property: Neem's anti-inflammatory property reduces redness and swelling associated with acne, making it an effective treatment for acne-prone skin.
Antioxidant property: Neem's antioxidant property helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage the skin, promoting healthy skin cell growth and reducing the appearance of acne scars.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Azadirachta indica, also known as neem, creams helps with acne because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as nimbin and nimandial, work to reduce the growth of acne-causing bacteria, including P. acnes. Additionally, neem creams have a soothing effect on the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with acne. This natural remedy also helps to unclog pores and prevent future breakouts, making it an effective and natural solution for managing acne.
remedy_constituents: Azadirachtin: A triterpenoid compound that helps with acne by exhibiting strong anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, reducing the growth of acne-causing bacteria and inflammation.

Quercetin: A flavonoid phenolic compound that helps with acne by acting as a potent antioxidant, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes that contribute to acne development.

Salannin: A neem-derived phenolic compound that helps with acne by displaying antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, reducing bacterial load and inflammation associated with acne, and promoting wound healing.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used to make creams for acne due to their high content of neem oil, which has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Used to make creams for acne due to their ability to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections.
Seeds: Used to make creams for acne due to their neem oil, which has antiseptic properties that help to prevent the spread of acne-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of azadirachta indica leaves and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried azadirachta indica leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of the azadirachta indica powder with 50 grams of cocoa butter and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and pour it into a glass jar, allowing it to cool and solidify for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's antioxidant compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the skin, which can contribute to acne development.

Bacterial inhibition: The antibacterial properties of ginger, specifically its ability to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, can help prevent acne by reducing the amount of bacteria that can clog pores and cause breakouts.

Anti-irritant and soothing properties: Ginger's anti-irritant compounds can help reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with acne, providing a calming effect on the skin and promoting a more balanced complexion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause acne. Additionally, ginger has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and fighting bacteria, ginger creams can help to clear up acne and prevent future breakouts, leaving the skin looking clearer and more balanced.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds, particularly [6]-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in combating acne-causing bacteria and reducing acne severity.

Curcuminoids: Although not directly derived from ginger, curcuminoids (such as curcumin) often co-extract with gingerols and shogaols in creams. They have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to acne.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne.
Roots: Contain antioxidants and antibacterial properties that aid in soothing and calming the skin, reducing the occurrence of acne.
Buds: High in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 zingiber officinale roots with a sharp knife and clean them thoroughly with water.
Chop the zingiber officinale roots into small pieces and mix with 250ml of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2-3 hours or until the oil turns golden brown and has a strong aroma.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Allow the zingiber officinale cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2-3 hours before use.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lavender's antibacterial properties help combat Pseudomonas aeruginosa, a bacteria commonly found in acne-causing lesions, reducing inflammation and preventing further breakouts.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with acne, allowing the skin to heal faster.
Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells, promoting healthy skin and reducing oxidative stress that can contribute to acne development.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with acne because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in the lavender plant, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to reduce inflammation and combat bacteria that can cause acne. Additionally, lavender's soothing and calming effects can help to reduce stress and promote a healthy balance of the skin's natural oils, leading to clearer and more even-toned skin. This makes lavender a popular natural remedy for acne-prone skin.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Linalool is a terpene that helps to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth, making it effective in treating acne caused by bacterial infections.

Lavandulifolioside: Lavandulifolioside is a phenolic compound that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of acne by protecting the skin from oxidative stress and inflammation.

Linalyl acetate: Linalyl acetate is a terpene that has antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause acne, and also has anti-inflammatory effects that reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used because they contain high levels of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they possess antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation.

Essential oil extracted from Flowers: Some of the key compounds in Lavender essential oil (extracted from flowers) like Linalool, Linalyl acetate, have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial and antifungal properties which may help reduce acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers that are free from contaminants and pesticides.
Infuse the dried flowers in 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at room temperature for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Mix 100g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter with the infused oil in a double boiler at 60°c for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Herbal teas like green tea, chamomile, and lavender contain antibacterial compounds that help kill bacteria that can cause acne, reducing inflammation and preventing further breakouts.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal teas rich in antioxidants like rosehip, calendula, and peppermint can reduce redness and swelling associated with acne by combating free radicals and soothing the skin.

Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants present in herbal teas like ginger, turmeric, and hibiscus help neutralize damage caused by environmental stressors and toxins, promoting healthy skin cell growth and reducing the appearance of acne scars.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, creams helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's ability to soothe and calm irritated skin reduces redness and swelling associated with acne. The antibacterial properties of Camellia sinensis also help to combat bacterial overgrowth, a common cause of acne. Additionally, the cream's hydrating properties help to balance the skin's pH, reducing the likelihood of clogged pores and breakouts. This natural remedy can be a gentle and effective solution for acne-prone skin.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Catechins, a type of phenolic compound, help with acne by exerting anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which reduce the severity of acne lesions.

Theaflavins: Theaflavins, a type of polyphenol, help with acne by inhibiting the production of sebum, a key factor in acne development, and also possessing antioxidant properties to reduce oxidative stress.

Epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG): EGCG, a type of catechin, helps with acne by reducing inflammation, modulating the immune system, and possessing antimicrobial properties to prevent the growth of acne-causing bacteria.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
Flowers: Utilized for their astringent properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent further breakouts.
Buds: Employed for their antimicrobial properties, which can help combat acne-causing bacteria and promote a healthy skin balance.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 teaspoons of camellia sinensis leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release their properties.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a clean container to remove solids.
Combine 1 cup of the camellia sinensis infusion with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax has melted and emulsified with the oil.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for about 30 minutes before transferring it to a storage container.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compound curcumin helps reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation associated with acne, allowing for faster healing and reduced scarring.

Antibacterial: The antibacterial properties of turmeric cream can combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that clog pores and cause breakouts, preventing further infection and reducing the severity of acne.

Anti-oxidant: Turmeric's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to acne, promoting healthy skin cell turnover and protecting against environmental stressors.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The active compound, curcumin, has been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause acne. It also has antioxidant properties that help to neutralize free radicals, which can damage the skin and contribute to acne. Additionally, curcuma longa creams can help to regulate the skin's natural balance, reducing the occurrence of breakouts and promoting a clear, healthy complexion. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to treat various skin conditions.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce acne inflammation and prevent further breakouts.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, another curcuminoid, has been shown to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria associated with acne, thereby preventing the spread of acne.
Turmerone: Turmerone, a sesquiterpene, has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help reduce acne severity and prevent infection.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that helps in soothing and treating acne.
Roots: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help in reducing redness and preventing bacterial growth that can lead to acne.
Leaves: They are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in soothing and calming the skin, reducing acne symptoms and promoting healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500g of curcuma longa rhizomes and 200ml of coconut oil for the base ingredients.
Grate 50g of the curcuma longa rhizomes and mix with 100g of beeswax for a smooth consistency.
Combine 250ml of coconut oil and 100g of shea butter in a double boiler to melt the mixture.
Add 10ml of vitamin e oil and 50g of the grated curcuma longa rhizomes to the melted mixture for preservation and potency.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify completely before transferring it to a clean container for storage.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sandalwood's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as beta-sitosterol, help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, soothing the skin and promoting a calm complexion.
Antibacterial: The antimicrobial properties of sandalwood, particularly its sesquiterpenes, inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause acne, reducing inflammation and preventing future breakouts.
Wound healing: Sandalwood's wound-healing properties, attributed to its triterpenoids, accelerate the repair process by promoting collagen synthesis, improving skin elasticity, and reducing scarring.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Santalum album, also known as sandalwood, creams helps with acne because of its unique composition and therapeutic properties. The cream's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with acne. Additionally, the antioxidants present in sandalwood cream help to protect the skin from environmental stressors and promote collagen production, leading to a smoother and more even-toned complexion. This results in a reduction in acne breakouts and an overall improvement in skin health.
remedy_constituents: Santalol: A terpene that has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce the growth of acne-causing bacteria and soothe inflamed skin.
Santalenic acid: A phenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with acne.
Linalool: A terpene that has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the growth of acne-causing bacteria and soothe inflamed skin.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes: Underground stems that store nutrients, are used to make creams for acne due to their high concentration of santalol, which has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Leaves: These are used in creams for acne due to their ability to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Buds: Buds: Buds are used in creams for acne due to their high concentration of essential oils, which have antibacterial and antifungal properties that help to combat acne-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Gather 50g of dried santalum album powder and 25g of candelilla wax for the cream base mixture preparation.
Combine the santalum album powder with 10ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler and heat until dissolved.
Add 20g of beeswax to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated and melted.
Remove the double boiler from heat and stir in 5ml of vitamin e oil and 2ml of lavender essential oil.
Pour the mixture into a tin container and allow to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Sage cream's antibacterial properties help to reduce the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a type of bacteria that can cause acne, by inhibiting their ability to multiply and produce toxins.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal sage creams have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne, making them an effective treatment for inflammatory acne types such as papules and pustules.

Antioxidant properties: Sage cream's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals in the skin, which can damage skin cells and contribute to acne development. By reducing oxidative stress, sage cream may help to prevent acne formation and promote healthy skin.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The herb's extracts have been found to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of acne-causing bacteria, such as P. acnes. Additionally, sage creams contain antioxidants that help protect the skin from environmental stressors and promote the healing of acne lesions. By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth, sage creams can help to clear up acne and promote a healthier, more balanced complexion.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are key factors in the development and progression of acne.
Carnosic acid: A phenolic diterpene that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
Ursolic acid: A triterpenoid that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce bacterial growth and inflammation associated with acne.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties to help soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
Stems: Utilized for their antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the skin.
Flowers: Employed for their antiseptic and antibacterial properties to combat acne-causing bacteria and promote a healthy skin environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has dried completely to preserve potency.
Chop 1/4 cup of fresh salvia officinalis leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter.
Combine the chopped herb with 2 tablespoons of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 hours or overnight at a low temperature of 100-150°f to allow infusion.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a clean glass container to discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with acne because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The thymol present in thyme has been shown to effectively combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that cause breakouts, reducing inflammation and redness associated with acne-prone skin. Additionally, thyme's antiseptic qualities help to prevent the spread of bacteria, promoting a clearer complexion. By soothing and calming the skin, herbal thyme creams can also reduce acne scars and hyperpigmentation, leaving skin looking healthier and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Thyme contains compounds like thymol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), the bacteria responsible for causing acne.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's essential oils, such as carvacrol and linalool, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidant compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to acne development.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and antibacterial properties, making them effective in treating acne-causing bacteria.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome of thyme contains bioactive compounds that have antifungal and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest thymus vulgaris leaves and dry them using a warm air circulation dryer for 2 hours at 30°c.
Grind dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of powdered thymus vulgaris with 20 grams of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass beaker.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 30 minutes, then remove from heat and let it cool to room temperature.
Whip the cooled mixture using an electric whisk for 10 minutes until it thickens into a creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with acne because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples. The herb licorice root extract also exhibits antimicrobial activity, combating bacteria that can cause breakouts. Additionally, licorice creams often contain soothing aloe vera and tea tree oil, which calm irritated skin and promote healing. By addressing both inflammation and bacterial imbalance, herbal licorice creams provide a comprehensive approach to acne treatment, helping to clear up blemishes and leave skin looking smoother and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce redness and swelling associated with acne by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Flavonoids: Herbal licorice creams contain flavonoids, such as glabridin and isoliquiritigenin, which have been shown to inhibit the activity of 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone linked to acne.

Saponins: Saponins in herbal licorice creams have antibacterial properties, which can help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that contribute to acne by reducing inflammation and preventing the spread of infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain compounds that help to soothe and calm inflammation, making them effective in reducing acne redness and swelling.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the licorice plant are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and can help to reduce acne by minimizing skin irritation.
remedy_recipe: Extract dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 1:2 ratio with ethanol for 24 hours at room temperature.
Filter the extracted mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Combine the filtered liquid with distilled water and glycerin in a 1:1:0.5 ratio by weight and stir well.
Heat the mixture slowly to 70°c over 30 minutes while stirring occasionally then cool to room temperature.
Fill sterilized jars with the prepared cream and seal tightly immediately for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with acne because they contain antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and antibacterial properties that work together to combat the underlying causes of acne. The cream's astringent and antiseptic properties help to dry out pimples and reduce redness, while its antibacterial agents kill bacteria that can cause acne. Additionally, dandelion's natural soothing properties calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with acne, promoting a clearer complexion.
Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents in dandelion cream combat P. acnes bacteria, which is a common cause of acne, helping to prevent breakouts and reduce the appearance of acne scars.
Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to acne, while also soothing and protecting the skin from further irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Leaves: The leaves contain antioxidants that help combat free radicals, which can contribute to skin damage and inflammation caused by acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and chop them into small pieces to facilitate drying.
Dry the chopped leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 6 hours or until they reach a crumbly texture.
Combine 20g of dried taraxacum officinale with 30g of cold-pressed coconut oil and blend until a smooth paste forms.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 70°c for 30 minutes to activate the herbal properties and thicken the cream.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature before transferring it to sterilized containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek creams helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with breakouts. The cream's antimicrobial compounds also inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P.acnes) bacteria, a common cause of acne. Additionally, fenugreek's antioxidant properties help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin cell regeneration. By addressing multiple factors contributing to acne, fenugreek creams provide a comprehensive solution for clearer, healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Fenugreek cream's antibacterial properties help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a type of bacteria that contributes to acne development, reducing inflammation and preventing further infection.

Anti-inflammatory property: Fenugreek's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as galactomannan and saponins, soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne, allowing for faster healing and reduced scarring.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in fenugreek cream help neutralize free radicals that can damage the skin, causing premature aging and exacerbating acne symptoms, promoting a more even-toned and radiant complexion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilage, a natural thickening agent that helps soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
Seeds: Fenugreek seeds contain galactomannan, a polysaccharide with anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried fenugreek seeds from a reputable source and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 20g of the ground fenugreek powder with 100g of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a small bowl until well combined.
Add 10ml of beeswax to the mixture and stir gently over low heat for 10 minutes or until fully melted and incorporated.
Remove from heat and add 2 drops of lavender essential oil for its calming properties, stirring well to combine.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel creams helps with acne because of its natural astringent and antibacterial properties. The cream's active ingredient, witch hazel, has been used for centuries to reduce inflammation and shrink the size of pores, allowing for a clearer complexion. Additionally, its antibacterial properties help to combat acne-causing bacteria, reducing the occurrence of new breakouts. As a result, witch hazel creams can effectively soothe and calm acne-prone skin, promoting a more balanced and radiant appearance.
remedy_properties: Tannins: Tannins have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce acne-causing bacteria on the skin and reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.

Vitamin A: Vitamin A has antioxidant and keratolytic properties, which help to unclog pores by breaking down dead skin cells and reducing sebum production, thus preventing clogged pores and blackheads.

Astringent compounds: The astringent compounds present in Witch Hazel have a drying effect on the skin, helping to reduce excess oil and tighten pores, making it an effective treatment for oily skin and acne-prone areas.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Witch Hazel plant contain tannins and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing redness and preventing bacterial growth associated with acne.
Bark: The bark of the Witch Hazel plant is rich in tannins and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and prevent scarring caused by acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh witch hazel leaves and flowers and dry them using shade drying method for 2 days.
Combine dried witch hazel leaves and flowers with 20% glycerin and 80% distilled water in a glass jar and let it steep for 4 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another glass container and discard solids, then add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and heat the mixture to melt the wax.
Pour the melted wax mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool to room temperature before sealing and storing in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.
Apply a thin layer of the cream to affected areas 2-3 times daily as needed, or after cleansing and toning.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The herb's antioxidants also help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples and breakouts. Additionally, chamomile has been shown to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria that can contribute to acne formation. As a result, using a chamomile cream as part of your skincare routine can help reduce the appearance of acne and promote healthier, clearer skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, making it an effective treatment for reducing inflammation.

Antibacterial property: Chamomile cream's antibacterial property inhibits the growth of bacteria such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), which is a common cause of acne, helping to prevent further breakouts.

Soothing and calming property: Chamomile cream's soothing and calming properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate acne, while also providing relief from itching and irritation caused by acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, chamomile roots help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and redness associated with acne.
Flowers: The delicate petals of chamomile flowers contain apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that gently calms the skin, reducing acne's redness and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers from a reputable supplier for infusion.
Combine the dried flowers with 500ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm place for 48 hours, shaking the jar occasionally to ensure even infusion.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding solids.
Allow the liquid to mature for 24 hours before whipping with beeswax and vitamin e oil to create a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with acne because it contains compounds that reduce inflammation, combat bacteria, and regulate sebum production. The antioxidant properties of yarrow help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with acne. Additionally, the antibacterial properties of yarrow cream target Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, which are a common cause of acne, thereby preventing breakouts and promoting clear skin. This natural solution can help to reduce acne symptoms and promote healthy-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Yarrow's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), which can contribute to acne breakouts.
Anti-inflammatory Properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne, helping to calm the skin and promote healing.
Antioxidant Properties: Yarrow's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from environmental stressors and free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to acne development.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, yarrow roots are used to create anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial creams that help soothe and heal acne-prone skin.

Flowers: With their delicate petals containing flavonoids and volatile oils, yarrow flowers are added to creams to provide antioxidant and antibacterial properties, reducing inflammation and combating acne-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and dry them in shade for at least two weeks or by air drying.
Combine one part dried achillea millefolium leaves with four parts coconut oil and blend until well combined.
Add two tablespoons of beeswax pellets to the mixture and stir over low heat for 10 minutes.
Remove from heat and let it cool until the mixture thickens then whip with a hand mixer for five minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and store in refrigerator at a temperature below 40°f for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with acne because they contain potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation caused by acne. The natural ingredients in elder cream, such as flavonoids and terpenes, also have antibacterial and antifungal properties that combat the bacteria and fungal infections that can contribute to acne. Additionally, elder creams can help to hydrate the skin, unclog pores, and reduce sebum production, all of which can help to prevent and treat acne-prone skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elderberry's antioxidant compounds help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to acne development by protecting the skin from damage caused by free radicals.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in elderberry creams, such as flavonoids and anthocyanins, help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with acne, while also soothing and calming the skin.

Antimicrobial properties: Elderberry's antimicrobial compounds, including phenolic acids and quercetin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria that contributes to acne development.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the elder plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming acne-prone skin.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the elder plant contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant and antibacterial properties that help to combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry sambucus nigra flowers, ensuring optimal potency for future use.
Combine 20 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers with 100 ml of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes at 60°c to facilitate the extraction of active compounds.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard solids, then add 10% vitamin e oil as preservative.
Whip 200 grams of beeswax with 100 ml of the sambucus nigra-infused oil mixture until a smooth, creamy texture is achieved.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for acne are topical creams made from plant extracts that help to reduce acne symptoms by soothing, calming, and protecting the skin. They are a natural alternative to conventional acne treatments and have been found to be effective in reducing inflammation, redness, and the occurrence of acne. The best herbal creams for acne include Melaleuca alternifolia, Calendula officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Silybum marianum, Saponaria officinalis, Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Aloe vera, Lavandula angustifolia, and Hypericum perforatum. If left untreated, acne can lead to low self-esteem, depression, and anxiety, negatively impacting daily life, relationships, and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to acne that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, rosacea, and seborrheic dermatitis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and calm the skin. They can also reduce redness and inflammation, promoting healthier-looking skin. Additionally, herbal creams may be used to treat other conditions such as psoriasis, keratosis pilaris, and folliculitis.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with acne is a blend of Aloe Vera, Tea Tree Oil, and Chamomile. Aloe Vera soothes and calms the skin, while Tea Tree Oil reduces inflammation and fights bacteria that cause acne. Chamomile, rich in antioxidants, gently exfoliates and reduces redness. Apply a thin layer of Aloe Vera as a base, followed by a few drops of Tea Tree Oil, and finish with a layer of Chamomile-infused cream for a well-balanced treatment that promotes healthy skin and reduces acne breakouts.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM, herbal creams have been found to be effective in treating acne due to the presence of medicinal plants and phytochemicals that significantly reduce inflammatory and noninflammatory acne lesions. The study, which systematically evaluated the efficacy and safety of medicinal plants and phytochemicals in the treatment of acne vulgaris, found that most trials showed a significant reduction in acne lesions and a relevant effect on acne severity, with some medicinal plants even revealing equal or higher efficacy to standard treatments. Overall, this suggests that herbal creams are a promising strategy for treating mild to moderate acne vulgaris, with a favorable safety profile and minimal adverse events.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with acne because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active ingredient, tea tree oil, has been shown to reduce the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a common bacteria that causes acne. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. By targeting the underlying causes of acne, Melaleuca alternifolia creams can help to clear pores and promote a healthier complexion, making it a popular natural remedy for acne-prone skin. Regular use can lead to a significant reduction in acne breakouts.

Calendula officinalis creams helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in Calendula, such as triterpenoids and saponins, work to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, while also inhibiting the growth of bacteria that can cause breakouts. Additionally, Calendula's ability to promote wound healing and tissue repair helps to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of acne and promoting a more even-toned complexion. This makes Calendula a popular natural remedy for acne-prone skin.

Aloe barbadensis creams helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant soothes and calms the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with acne. Its ability to penetrate deep into the skin also helps to unclog pores and prevent future breakouts. Additionally, Aloe vera's antioxidant properties help to combat free radicals that can cause skin damage and promote healthy skin regeneration, making it an effective natural remedy for acne-prone skin.

Silybum marianum creams helps with acne because they contain silymarin, a potent antioxidant that soothes and calms the skin. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, promoting faster healing. Silybum marianum also has antibacterial properties that combat acne-causing bacteria, preventing further breakouts. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help balance the skin's natural pH, reducing irritation and promoting a healthy, radiant complexion, ultimately leading to clearer skin. This natural approach addresses acne from multiple angles.

Saponaria officinalis creams helps with acne because they contain natural saponins, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds work to reduce the amount of bacteria on the skin, thereby preventing clogged pores and breakouts. Additionally, Saponaria officinalis' anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with acne. The cream's ability to balance the skin's pH and regulate its natural moisture barrier also contributes to its acne-fighting benefits.

Echinacea purpurea creams helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream reduces redness and inflammation associated with acne, promoting a clear and even-toned complexion. Echinacea's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause skin damage and exacerbate acne. Additionally, its soothing and calming effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are known to trigger acne breakouts. By addressing these underlying causes, Echinacea purpurea creams can help to alleviate acne symptoms and promote healthier skin.

Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with acne because they contain the active compound glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the skin, making it less prone to acne breakouts. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has antibacterial properties that help to control the growth of bacteria that can cause acne. By reducing inflammation and bacterial growth, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help to clear up acne and promote healthy, clear skin. This can lead to a reduction in acne severity and frequency.

Aloe vera creams helps with acne because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel inside Aloe vera leaves contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene and other antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and combat bacteria that can cause acne. Additionally, Aloe vera has antibacterial properties that help to kill bacteria and prevent the spread of acne, making it a popular natural remedy for acne-prone skin. It also helps to reduce redness and promote healing.

Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The calming effects of the cream reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. The lavender oil present in the cream has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause acne, such as Propionibacterium acnes. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of the cream help to soothe and balance the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing, resulting in a clearer and more even-toned complexion. This makes Lavandula angustifolia creams a popular natural remedy for acne-prone skin.

Hypericum perforatum creams helps with acne because they contain active compounds like hyperforin and hypericin that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties help reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne, while also inhibiting the growth of bacteria that can cause acne breakouts. Additionally, Hypericum perforatum creams have antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy skin and reducing the appearance of acne.
